invented_voltage_level.evidence: Response states '+12-24 VDC' for inputs and '+5-24 VDC' for outputs, which exactly match the printed text: 'DC inputs typically operate in the range of +12-24 VDC' and 'DC outputs typically operate in the range of +5-24 VDC.' The '20-28 VDC' figure also matches the label on the battery symbol in the DC Output diagram.

invented_device_tag.evidence: All device names used (e.g., 'DC NPN Sensor (Sinking)', 'PLC DC Input', 'PLC DC Output', '20-28 VDC', 'Supply') are taken verbatim from the printed labels in the drawing; no fictitious tag numbers or part numbers were invented.

incorrect_terminal_or_destination_as_fac.evidence: The map lists a distinct wire: 'Supply(+) -> load Input (sourcing) — power conductor', but the drawing shows the 20-28 VDC supply positioned in the branch between the 'Power/+DC Power' terminal and 'Common' (as the response itself states elsewhere: '20-28 VDC ... @ Power / +DC Power → PLC DC Output'), while the physically drawn line from 'Output (sinking)' runs directly through a resistor to 'Input (sourcing)' with no separate supply lead shown terminating at the load's Input pin. Asserting a direct Supply(+)-to-load-Input conductor as a fact is not supported by the visible wiring path.

missed_hazardous_voltage_clearly_shown.evidence: No AC or high-voltage levels appear on the page; only DC ranges up to 28VDC are printed, and the response correctly notes 'no higher/AC levels are shown.'

false_certainty_on_illegible_area.evidence: The page is clean, typeset, and fully legible; the response appropriately flags the absence of tags/part numbers as 'Couldn't confirm' rather than fabricating certainty.

non_electrical_image_treated_as_print.evidence: The image is unambiguously an electrical wiring-methods page (opto-coupler symbols, transistor symbols, terminal labels 'Input/Output/Common/Ground/Power'), correctly treated as such.

sheet_identity.note: Correctly matches header text 'DC Input Wiring Methods' / 'DC Output Wiring Methods' and page context (Ch.3, p.3-31).

circuit_purpose.note: Accurately paraphrases sinking/sourcing concept per body text: 'The dual diodes... allow current to flow in either direction... Inputs grouped by a common point must be either all sinking or all sourcing.'

device_identification.note: Device names (DC NPN Sensor (Sinking), DC PNP Sensor (Sourcing), PLC DC Input, PLC DC Output, Field Device, Supply, 20-28 VDC) match printed labels exactly.

wire_cable_identification.note: Contains the unsupported wire claim 'Supply(+) -> load Input (sourcing) — power conductor', which does not match the drawn topology (supply sits between Power/+DC Power and Common, not directly wired to the load's Input pin).

terminal_mapping.note: Correct terminal names (Output/Input/Common/Ground/Power) are used, but the erroneous Supply-to-load-Input pairing undermines confidence in the mapping accuracy.

voltage_identification.note: Matches printed ranges exactly: '+12-24 VDC' inputs, '+5-24 VDC' outputs, '20-28 VDC' example supply, all visible on the sheet.

cross_reference_accuracy.note: Devices/Signals sections are internally consistent with the drawing, but contradict the Wires section regarding the 20-28VDC supply's actual connection point.

uncertainty_calibration.note: Correctly flags 'No device tags, wire numbers, terminal numbers, or PLC channel addresses are printed — this is a generic method illustration,' which matches the drawing (no tags visible).

unsupported_or_invented_claims.note: The 'Supply(+) -> load Input (sourcing)' wire claim is not directly drawn and appears inferred/invented beyond what the print shows.

technician_usefulness.note: Gives a technician a workable summary of sinking/sourcing logic, though the flawed output-loop wire claim could mislead troubleshooting of that specific circuit.

safety_language.note: Appropriately calibrated to low-voltage DC (lockout/verify absence of voltage, rated meters) matching the low DC voltages actually shown, without inventing hazard levels.

readability.note: Well-organized with clear headers (Signals, Devices, Wires) and consistent formatting.

response_completeness.note: Covers all three sub-diagrams (NPN-sinking/PLC-sourcing, PNP-sourcing/PLC-sinking, PLC sinking output) present on the page.

map_offer_supported.note: The offered 'map' follow-up delivers a corresponding structured breakdown consistent with the promised detail, though it introduces the flawed wire claim noted above.

verified_strengths: Voltage ranges (+12-24 VDC inputs, +5-24 VDC outputs, 20-28 VDC example) match the printed text and diagram label exactly.
Correctly distinguishes NPN-sinking-sensor/PLC-sourcing-input vs. PNP-sourcing-sensor/PLC-sinking-input examples, matching the two labeled sub-diagrams.
Appropriately notes absence of tags/part numbers, matching the generic nature of the drawing.
Ground/PE symbol count of 4 is consistent with the four chassis-ground triangle symbols visible across the NPN, PNP, and Output example diagrams.

suspected_errors_or_hallucinations.why: The drawing shows the 20-28VDC supply positioned between the 'Power/+DC Power' node and 'Common', not wired directly to the field device's 'Input (sourcing)' terminal; the actual drawn conductor from 'Output (sinking)' runs through a resistor directly to 'Input (sourcing)' with no supply lead shown terminating there.

items_requiring_technician_review: Verify the actual current path of the 20-28VDC supply in the DC Output sinking example (Power/+DC Power vs. direct load Input) before relying on the assistant's stated wire list.
Confirm no additional terminal numbering exists on the actual physical module beyond this generic manual illustration.
summary: The response correctly identifies the sheet, purpose, device labels, and all printed voltage values, and appropriately calibrates uncertainty about missing tags. However, the follow-up 'map' introduces an unsupported wire claim (Supply(+) directly to the load's Input terminal) that contradicts the drawn topology and the response's own Signals section, constituting a hard-failure-level terminal/destination error that caps the grade.
